meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to use SaaS pricing strategy, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship profitable apps faster than ever as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: Project abandonment stems from scope creep and undefined value. By implementing high-ticket, tiered SaaS pricing models early, you force scope discipline. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement to ensure every feature you build is tied directly to a paid customer requirement, guaranteeing project completion.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished projects is paved with good intentions and endless feature lists. As a freelancer, you know the feeling: the initial spark of an idea fades into a slog of unoptimized code and scope creep, leaving you with nothing but a half-baked repository and a dwindling bank account. You aren't failing because you lack skill; you are failing because you lack a framework that forces your development to align with market value.

True mastery isn't about writing more code—it's about writing the right code that pays the bills. By shifting your mindset from 'developer' to 'product architect' and leveraging high-leverage tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can transform your workflow. Stop chasing features and start building assets. Here is how you ship, scale, and succeed.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, define three pricing tiers that dictate your MVP scope. This prevents scope creep by limiting features to specific price points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate a tiered pricing structure for a [Niche] application. Define 3 tiers: Basic (core utility), Pro (automation/integration), and Enterprise (custom/security). Ensure the MVP only contains the Basic tier features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project requirements doc based on the generated pricing tiers.'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready boilerplate that mirrors your Tier 1 requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS boilerplate using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include authentication and a billing setup that supports the previously defined Tier 1 structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure and configure the Stripe integration for the Basic tier.'

steps.title: Implement Pricing-Driven Auth
steps.description: Connect your authentication layer to your pricing logic so that users are gated based on their subscription status immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write middleware for Next.js that checks user subscription status in Supabase. Redirect users to the pricing page if they attempt to access features above their tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the subscription guard middleware and test the redirect flow.'

steps.title: Automate Feature Deployment
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build out specific features only after the pricing logic is locked in, ensuring zero wasted effort.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API endpoint for the 'Pro' tier feature: [Feature Name]. Ensure it validates the user's subscription status before executing the logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add the Pro feature endpoint and unit test the subscription validation.'

steps.title: Final Review and Ship
steps.description: Audit the project for performance and ensure the billing flow is airtight before handing it off to the client or launching.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a security and performance audit on the current codebase. Identify any bottlenecks in the subscription handling logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run full test suite and prepare the production build for deployment.'

pro_tips: Never build a feature that isn't mapped to a revenue tier; if it doesn't have a price tag, it's a distraction.
Use Claude Code's 'review' flag to force an architectural critique of your code before pushing to production.
Implement 'Feature Flags' early so you can toggle features on/off for specific tiers without rewriting code.
Always include a 'Contact Sales' button for the Enterprise tier as a placeholder to gauge interest before building expensive custom features.
Treat your codebase as a product—use semantic versioning so you can track how your pricing model evolves alongside your features.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel, set up your Stripe dashboard, and conduct a soft launch to your first 5 potential clients to validate the pricing tiers.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your best ideas rot in unfinished repositories. By aligning your pricing strategy with your technical execution, you create a repeatable system that turns code into profit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
